Title: Genome-Wide Identification of the Bcl-2 Associated Athanogene (BAG) Gene Family in Solanum lycopersicum and the Functional Role of SlBAG9 in Response to Osmotic Stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional characterization of SlBAG9 in this study is entirely based on heterologous expression in Arabidopsis thaliana, although SlBAG9 is a tomato gene. Previous work (cited within this article) showed SlBAG9 as a negative regulator of thermotolerance, but high-temperature phenotypes are not newly characterized in the present Results section.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. SlBAG9 is a tomato BAG family protein containing a BAG domain and N-terminal IQ calmodulin-binding motif, identified and cloned based on the SL4.0/ITAG4.0 genome.
2. SlBAG9 shows organ- and stress-specific expression, with high transcript levels in red fruits and responsiveness to multiple abiotic stresses and ABA.
3. Heterologous overexpression of SlBAG9 in Arabidopsis significantly reduces seed germination and seedling growth under mannitol, NaCl, and ABA, indicating that SlBAG9 negatively regulates osmotic stress and ABA tolerance.
4. SlBAG9-overexpressing plants exhibit attenuated induction of ABA/stress-responsive genes (ABI3, RD29A, DREB2A, P5CS1) and ROS-scavenging genes (FSD1, CAT1), lower SOD and CAT activities, higher H2O2 and MDA levels, and aggravated oxidative damage under stress, linking SlBAG9 to ABA signaling and antioxidant defense regulation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous overexpression of tomato SlBAG9 in Arabidopsis negatively regulates osmotic stress tolerance, increasing sensitivity to drought- and salt-induced osmotic stress and to ABA during seed germination and seedling growth.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Under mannitol, NaCl, and ABA treatments, SlBAG9 overexpression attenuates transcriptional upregulation of ABA-responsive genes (ABI3, RD29A) and stress-responsive genes (DREB2A, P5CS1) as well as ROS-scavenging genes (FSD1, CAT1), resulting in lower SOD and CAT activities, excessive H2O2 accumulation, enhanced lipid peroxidation (MDA), and thus increased oxidative damage and stress sensitivity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in ABA-mediated drought and salt stress signaling and ROS scavenging pathways, where SlBAG9 overexpression dampens induction of ABA/stress-responsive genes and ROS-detoxifying genes, leading to reduced antioxidant defense under osmotic and ABA stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: SlBAG9 --(negative regulation of expression under osmotic/ABA stress)--> ABI3, RD29A, DREB2A, P5CS1, FSD1, CAT1.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bcl-2-associated athanogene (BAG) family protein containing a C-terminal BAG domain and an N-terminal IQ calmodulin-binding motif unique to plant BAGs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In tomato, SlBAG9 shows highest transcript levels in red fruits and is induced by high temperature; its expression generally decreases under drought and H2O2 treatment and is downregulated by H2O2 while responsive to various abiotic stresses and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Tomato Genome version SL4.0 / ITAG4.0 annotation
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic stress simulated by mannitol (200 and 300 mM), salt stress by NaCl (175 and 200 mM), ABA treatment (1.0 and 1.5 μM) during Arabidopsis seed germination and seedling growth; tomato seedlings treated with PEG6000 drought (20%), 100 mM NaCl, high temperature (42 °C), cold (4 °C), 50 μM cadmium, 100 μM ABA, and 10 mM H2O2.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (osmotic stress, drought, salt, ABA signaling, oxidative stress) and antioxidant defense.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress: osmotic stress (drought and salt), ABA sensitivity, and oxidative stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 300 mM mannitol, ~85% of WT seeds germinated after 5 days, compared with <60% (line 2-12) and <30% (line 4-9) in SlBAG9-overexpressing lines; after 10 days on 300 mM mannitol, >90% of WT cotyledons had turned green versus <15% in transgenic lines. Under 1.5 μM ABA, 100% of WT seeds but ~40% of transgenic seeds germinated after 5 days; >80% WT versus <50% transgenic cotyledons turned green after 7 days.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: SlBAG9-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines show reduced seed germination rate and cotyledon greening under mannitol, NaCl, and ABA; increased accumulation of H2O2 and MDA and reduced SOD and CAT activities under drought, salt, and ABA; and attenuated induction of stress/ABA-responsive genes (ABI3, RD29A, DREB2A, P5CS1) and ROS-scavenging genes (FSD1, CAT1) under osmotic and ABA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum cv. Ailsa Craig) seedlings for SlBAG gene expression analysis; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia-0 (WT) and SlBAG9-overexpressing lines 2-12 and 4-9 for functional assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide BAG-domain HMMER and BLASTP searches; cloning of full-length CDS; qRT-PCR for organ- and stress-specific SlBAG expression; seed germination and seedling growth assays on 1/2 MS with mannitol, NaCl, and ABA; measurement of H2O2 (titanium peroxide complex at 415 nm), MDA (thiobarbituric acid assay), and SOD and CAT activities; statistical analyses of biological replicates using 2^-ΔΔCT for gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression of SlBAG9 in Arabidopsis Col-0 (stable homozygous lines 2-12 and 4-9), phenotypic assays of seed germination and seedling growth under mannitol, NaCl, and ABA; qRT-PCR of stress/ABA-responsive and ROS-scavenging genes; biochemical assays of H2O2 and MDA contents and SOD and CAT enzyme activities under stress treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in a genome-wide BAG-domain (PF02179) HMMER search and BLASTP screen of the tomato ITAG4.0 proteome, validated against NCBI, and full-length CDS cloned to confirm gene structure.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: SlBAG9 functions as a negative regulator of drought-, salt-, and ABA-induced osmotic stress tolerance; reducing its expression or activity in tomato could enhance tolerance by promoting expression of stress/ABA-responsive and ROS-scavenging genes and strengthening antioxidant defenses. SlBAG9 and its expression patterns under stress may serve as molecular markers or targets in tomato resistance breeding programs aimed at improving abiotic stress and oxidative stress tolerance.
